title: Opportunity Brief No. 001 · Agentic Onboarding & Employee Events
summary:
Full opportunity brief on the agentic-onboarding category — the market evidence under the Tenure decision (Briefing 016). The argumentative spine: onboarding is not a module, employee events are a category. At 100,000 employees there are roughly 144,000 events per year, 395 per day; 8,000 joiners (5.6%), 30,000 internal moves (~21%), 6,000 exits (~4%), 100,000 anniversaries (~69%). The HCM suites have built products for the 5.6%.

The brief covers: (a) market shape across three axes — suite-bundled onboarding (Workday, Rippling, UKG, BambooHR, Paylocity, Paycom, HiBob, SAP, ADP), experience-first standalones (Enboarder, Click Boarding, Sapling, HROnboard, Talmundo, Appical), and compliance specialists (WorkBright, HireRight, Sterling, iCIMS, DocuSign, Tracker); (b) five customer-complaint patterns expanded with G2/Capterra/Reddit signal — the Day-1 cliff, the internal-move blind spot, the leaver problem, the anniversary tax, the compliance-experience seam; (c) AI-native shift split between verified shipping (Workday Illuminate Sept 2025, Workday ASOR GA 2026, SAP Joule + Autonomous Suite May 2026, ADP Marketplace AI Agents March 2026, Rippling AI March 2026, Enboarder PowerfulAI April 2025, Deel AI Workforce Aug 2025, 15Five Kona+AMAYA) and announcement theater (Ashby Custom Agents beta, Greenhouse MCP unsubstantiated docs, SmartRecruiters 'autonomous TA' April 2026, ClearCo rebrand, iCIMS Sourcing Agent, HiBob's self-acknowledged AI gap, Eightfold lifecycle-thin); (d) five Stratum wedges Workday and Rippling structurally cannot say back — one agent set across the full lifecycle with decision-class typing, four MCP servers published and inspectable, governance as artifact (Mobley-grade evidence), people-graph integration on top of any HRIS in weeks not quarters, and the autonomous-operating proof; (e) buyer dynamics by employee band with the COO/CHRO/IT-Security triangle and which budget pays at each tier; (f) regulatory landscape covering I-9/E-Verify, EEO-1, EU AI Act Annex III, GDPR Article 22/17, NYC AEDT, state pay-transparency, Colorado SB 26-189, Texas TRAIGA, EEOC adverse-impact, and BIPA — what an events agent can and cannot do under each regime; (g) the 'moments that matter' framing as the report's argumentative spine; (h) sequenced recommended actions for this fortnight, next fortnight, and Q3 — owned by Compass, Vector+Cadence, Forge, Kernel, Helm, Tessera, and Eglin.

Strongest claim: nobody on the watch-list has shipped, to public-review evidence, an MCP-native, lifecycle-typed, decision-class-aware employee-events agent. Weakest claim: the standalone employee-events market will open when the lifecycle proves separable (category-formation prediction, suggestive not conclusive). Confidence: High. Alert level: L3 Action Recommended.
